_comment: Maps OpenAPI operationIds to n8n resources/operations. The generator reads this with vendor/sdk-generation/openapi/v1.0.0.yaml to produce nodes/Imagekit/resources/_generated/*.ts. Edit this file (not the generated TS) to change UI field names, descriptions, or operation grouping. Operations listed in `programmatic` are skipped by the generator and must be hand-written.

file._comment: Partial codegen: the 9 operations below are generated; `upload` and `update` remain hand-written under nodes/Imagekit/resources/file/{upload,update}.ts. The hand-written nodes/Imagekit/resources/file/index.ts splices both halves together so Imagekit.node.ts sees a single resource. defaultOperation is set to 'get' purely so the generated description type-checks; the splicer overrides the operation switcher with one that defaults to 'upload'.

_programmatic_comment: These operationIds are skipped by codegen. The hand-written asset resource and the file `upload`/`update` ops live under nodes/Imagekit/resources/<name>/ and are imported either directly (asset) or composed via a splicer (file). Migrate the rest by extending the generator (collection fields, derived values).

_triggers_comment: Webhook trigger nodes generated from the spec's top-level `webhooks:` section. Each entry produces one INodeType under nodes/Imagekit/triggers/_generated/<name>Trigger.node.ts. `events` lists the OpenAPI operationIds; the generator resolves each to the actual event-name path key (e.g. `file-created` -> `file.created`). All triggers share the hand-written verifySignature helper and the ImagekitWebhookApi credential.

file.description: Triggers when files are created, updated, or deleted in your ImageKit Media Library

fileVersion.description: Triggers when file versions are created or deleted

uploadPreTransform.description: Triggers on the outcome of pre-transformations applied during upload

uploadPostTransform.description: Triggers on the outcome of post-transformations applied after upload

videoTransformation.description: Triggers on the lifecycle events of a video transformation job
